SDG 8

Kadir Has University fosters inclusive economic growth and supports decent work opportunities through research, entrepreneurship, and student engagement. The University’s Teknopark and entrepreneurship programs encourage students and alumni to develop innovative solutions in the technology and creative industries. TÜBİTAK-funded projects explore new strategies for market development and sustainable business practices, while career workshops and internship programs connect students to local and international employers. Social responsibility courses and collaboration with municipal partners allow students to actively participate in projects that enhance employability and contribute to the local economy.

Occupational Health and Safety (ISG) Projects:

Kadir Has University Silivri Technopark was established in 2023 in partnership with Silivri Municipality and the Kadir Has Foundation, located in Selimpaşa, Silivri. It accepts companies working in its focus areas into the technology park and aims to contribute to the regional and national economy through R&D projects carried out under the University-Public-Industry-Civil Society quadruple helix model.

FabriKHAS aspires to become a unique creative hub for the screen industries, offering an ecosystem for collaboration, innovation, and professional development. It aims to foster synergy among professionals, strengthen creativity, and position itself as the heart of Istanbul’s screen industries.

Faculty: Faculty of Art and Design | Project Type: TÜBİTAK 1001 | Project Title: Gender Dynamics of Skilled Migrant Women’s Labor Migration and Urban Life Experiences: The Case of Istanbul | Program Code: 1001 | Project No: 221K001 | Project Leader: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Ezgi Tuncer | Scholarship Holders: Ela Gök, Liana Kuyumcuyan, Sare Nur Avcı – MA in Architecture and Urban Studies | Project Period: 01.04.2022 – 01.11.2023 | Summary: This project explores the socio-spatial dynamics shaping the migration, settlement, daily life practices, and urban experiences of highly educated (skilled) migrants living and working in Istanbul. It examines the migration reasons and processes of middle-class, transnational communities from the Global North and Global South, investigating their urban life experiences (home, work, and socialization) at the intersection of gender, nation, and ethnicity (identity and origin). The study highlights that these well-educated migrants, often perceived as a privileged “expat” group, are in fact transnational middle classes who may face precarity, economic crises, conflict, and repressive regimes. It aims to reveal the discriminatory practices they encounter in Istanbul and to contribute to the development of new urban and legal policies to improve their living conditions.

8.4. Courses

LAW 438 – International and European Labor Law (Elective)

Course Description: Covers the International Labour Organization (ILO), European Union, Council of Europe, European Convention on Human Rights (ECHR), and the European Social Charter, as well as Article 90/5 of the Turkish Constitution. Includes analysis of the European Court of Justice and European Court of Human Rights case law, along with decisions from ILO bodies related to labor relations.
